_comment: The Tier 1 auto-fix list (spec Section 6.2/6.3). This list is DATA, shipped from the repo, NEVER hand-edited on a box. Qualification bar for tier 1: the signature is unambiguous (a hard evidence predicate, not a heuristic), the fix has succeeded on a real incident at least once, the fix is reversible in one line, and the blast radius is the looping unit only. tier 2 = fix-with-operator-stamp (config-shape change); tier 3 = never-auto (propose and hold).

fix_classes.title: stale-lock removal (dead-pid-proven, JSON-parsed)
fix_classes.proof: the session-health failure defines the safe version (parse with a real parser; dead-pid proof required; never touch a live lock)

fix_classes.title: telegram offset rewind + channel restart
fix_classes.proof: shipped and running fleet-wide since v10.14.5 (telegram-offset-healthcheck.sh)

fix_classes.title: orphan-gateway clear + kickstart (marker archive -> port kill -> kickstart -> stability watch)
fix_classes.proof: the Box E verified 4-step procedure; kill-list contains ONLY the orphan pid

fix_classes.title: cron park on breaker trip (disable, never delete)
fix_classes.proof: the loop-detector / furnace-watch encoded rule (disable never delete)

fix_classes.title: light-context set on a bloating resume cron
fix_classes.proof: the encoded loop-detector fix (740,269 input tokens/tick -> 0)

fix_classes.title: process-unit park on process-breaker trip + boot-log capture
fix_classes.reversible_in: unpark the unit (single start + stability watch)
fix_classes.proof: stop-and-flag is strictly safer than the status quo (infinite respawn)

fix_classes.title: config restore from last-good snapshot on validate-fail (as box user)
fix_classes.reversible_in: the snapshot IS the revert; the prior file is itself snapshotted first

fix_classes.title: heartbeat allowlist flip on a confirmed idle-burn P1
fix_classes.reversible_in: restore the prior heartbeat block (snapshot revert line)
fix_classes.proof: applied twice (operator box, Box C). STARTS as Tier 2 on client boxes; graduates to Tier 1 per box AFTER the operator stamps it once (Open Decision T4).

tier_notes.1: AUTO-FIX: deterministic, proven, reversible; apply immediately, report after.
tier_notes.2: FIX WITH OPERATOR STAMP: the watchdog prepares the exact command + snapshot + revert line and sends it as a one-tap proposal to the OPERATOR (never the client); an `approve <finding-id>` reply executes it.
tier_notes.3: NEVER AUTO (propose and hold): anything touching a client's model choice (sovereignty is absolute), credentials, doctrine files, deletion of anything, ambiguous findings, and any fix whose verify failed once -> Rescue Rangers with the structured escalation format.
